data.title: mETH Protocol: cmETH withdrawals have been resumed, user funds are not affected
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Liquidity pledge/re-pledge protocol mETH Protocol announced on X platform that cmETH withdrawals have been resumed, user funds are safe and fully supported, and a detailed post-event analysis report will be released soon, outlining the course of the incident and all measures taken.
Earlier news, Mantle's liquidity pledge/re-pledge protocol mETH Protocol, which has a deep binding relationship with Bybit, announced the suspension of cmETH withdrawals after learning about the recent security incident involving certain mETH and cmETH transactions on Bybit, but deposit and pledge services are continuing as usual.

data.title: Arthur Hayes Calls for Ethereum Rollback After $1.4B Bybit Hack – Is ETH’s Reputation at Risk?
data.description:
The post Arthur Hayes Calls for Ethereum Rollback After $1.4B Bybit Hack – Is ETH’s Reputation at Risk? appeared first on Coinpedia Fintech News
The recent Bybit hack, which led to a loss of $1.4 billion, has raised serious concerns in the crypto space. The attackers managed to compromise Bybit’s ETH cold wallet, transferring the stolen funds into one address before spreading them across...
source: https://coinpedia.org/news/arthur-hayes-calls-for-ethereum-rollback-after-1-4b-bybit-hack-is-eths-reputation-at-risk/

data.title: Chainflip: Strengthening ETH broker-level screening mechanisms to reject suspicious deposits
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Chainflip updated the latest situation of the Bybit theft incident and said: "We have noticed that hackers are trying to convert Bybit's stolen funds into BTC through Chainflip. At present, we have shut down some front-end services to prevent the flow of funds, but because the protocol is completely decentralized and has 150 nodes, we cannot completely shut down the entire system. As a longer-term solution, we are strengthening the ETH broker-level screening mechanism to reject suspicious deposits through broker-api. Currently, the mechanism has been applied to BTC, and we only need to complete the implementation of ETH."

data.title: Currently, 5 institutions/individuals have provided loan support totaling 120,000 ETH to Bybit, totaling over 320 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, 5 institutions/individuals have provided a total of 120,000 ETH (US$320.97 million) in loan support to Bybit. Specifically: Bitget: 40,000 ETH (US$105.96 million); Institutions/whales withdrawing from Binance: 11,800 ETH (US$31.02 million); MEXC: 12,652 stETH (US$33.75 million); Binance or another institution/whale withdrawing from Binance: 36,000 ETH (US$96.54 million); 0x327...45b address: 20,000 ETH (US$53.7 million).

data.title: Orderly Network: Mantle on-chain deposits have been reopened
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Orderly Network announced that deposits on the Mantle chain have been reopened. Previously, due to the hacker attack on Bybit and a large amount of funds being transferred, Orderly temporarily suspended the deposit business on the Mantle chain.

data.title: Binance Market Update (2025-02-22)
data.description:
The global cryptocurrency market cap now stands at $3.25T, up by 1.64% over the last day, according to CoinMarketCap data.

Bitcoin (BTC) has been trading between $94,872 and $99,475 over the past 24 hours. As of 09:30 AM (UTC) today, BTC is trading at $96,359, down by -2.05%.

Most major cryptocurrencies by market cap are trading lower. Market outperformers include STMX, PNUT, and SOLV, up by 44%, 10%, and 10%, respectively.

Whales Accumulate 930,000 LTC Over Two Weeks

ZachXBT：Lazarus Group Transfers 5000 ETH Amid Bybit Incident

data.title: Mask Network founder: Some ETH has been deposited back to Bybit
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that Mask Network founder Suji Yan posted on the X platform that he had deposited some ETH back to Bybit, and there was probably less than 10 million US dollars in the public address, and he would continue to support it.
